text.tspan: Hybrid versus intrinsic Majorana nanowires,
a self-consistent Hartree-Fock-Bogoliubov analysis
a self-consistent Hartree-Fock-Bogoliubov analysis
Hybrid versus intrinsic Majorana nanowires,
Francisco Lobo, Elsa Prada, Pablo SanJose
Instituto de Ciencia de Materiales de Madrid (ICMM), CSIC, Madrid, Spain

tspan.tspan: We then specify this formalism for the case of a crystal system and an
atomistic electron-electron interaction, structuring the generalized
eigen-problem in terms of the Bloch momentum and spin degrees of
freedom. At last, we apply this theory to the case of hexagon boron
nitride structures in a nearest-neighbor tight-binding model for the
electronic Bloch states. We then solve the generalized eigen-problem
numerically and obtain the excitonic states energies and wave-
functions.

tspan.tspan: Structure of the generalized eigen-problem in a crystal in
terms of the Bloch momentum degrees of freedom.
We then specify this formalism for the case of a crystal system and an
atomistic electron-electron interaction, structuring the generalized
eigen-problem in terms of the Bloch momentum and spin degrees of
freedom. At last, we apply this theory to the case of hexagon boron
nitride structures in a nearest-neighbor tight-binding model for the
electronic Bloch states. We then solve the generalized eigen-problem
numerically and obtain the excitonic states energies and wave-
functions.

text.tspan: References
Hybrid vs Intrinsic results
Self-consistent superconductivity
Lutchyn, et al., Phy. Rev. Lett.
Prada, et al. Nature Rev.
Abstract
We theoretically investigated hybrid and intrinsic nanowires
accounting for self-consistent superconductive correlations (as
opposed to an extrinsically fixed pairing) in a Hartree-Fock-Bogoliubov
mean-field theory. For this, we Hartree-Fock decoupled a quartic

tspan.tspan: Hamiltonian in a Nambu-spinor scheme accounting for the anomalous

text.tspan: terms, extracting the mean-field self-energy as a spin-1/2 Hubbard
term in the Wannier limit. We discuss beyond minimal models how the
combined effect of spin-orbit coupling and Zeeman field tangled with
the emergent self-consistent superconductivity triggers a trivial-
topological phase transition, along with the role of finite length on the
emergence of Majorana oscillations. We show that Majoranas do still
emerge in the hybrid case, although under certain restrictions due to

tspan.tspan: not accounting for disorder (ballistic regime), but most importantly that

text.tspan: they emerge in a precarious balance in the intrinsic case, not being
fully topological protected.
Majorana minimal model
